action: Cycle-305 audit M1 (MEDIUM, shipped under WARN): malformed triage-decision.json must SURFACE, not silently fall back to prose. Both declaration-primary entry points swallow read errors today: triagecap.CommittedFloorCount (floors.go — `err == nil && ok` drops the error) and triagecap.DeferredFloorPackagesDecl (deferred.go — ignores ReadDeferredFloors error). Semantics to implement: ABSENT file or ABSENT field = legitimate fail-open fallback to prose (backward compat, keep); PRESENT-BUT-MALFORMED field = integrity violation — route an actionable correction through the existing correction ladder (the artifact producer owns the companion and can always fix its own JSON) or at minimum a loud reviewer WARN naming the parse error; never an indistinguishable silent downgrade. Rationale: a compromised/buggy artifact producer can write invalid JSON to defeat the declaration-primary guard and re-enter the prose path (the audit calls this the bypass vector; 'documented trade-off' does not eliminate it). TDD pins: malformed committed_floors AND malformed deferred_floors each produce a correction/WARN containing the JSON error; absent file/field still falls back silently; well-formed declarations unchanged.
